claim_text: Benchmark success depends not only on code synthesis quality but also on staying on task, managing work over many turns, and using evaluative feedback when it becomes visible.
mechanism_text: High non-finish and early-stop rates indicate that many failures arise before full task completion, the strong task_tracker correlation suggests organized work management is associated with better outcomes, and the large score gain when tests are revealed indicates that visible evaluative feedback improves search and correction during repository construction.
bounds: This is a bounded operational mechanism, not a clean causal proof; the task_tracker result is correlational, and the evidence does not separate model capability from agent-loop design, stopping policy, or other system-level choices.
